assertions.description: Output includes all required sections: Visual Position Statement, Five Dimensions, Reference Points, What This Visual Language Is Not, and AI Prompt Anchor
The visual position statement creates a specific visual world — not just mood words but evocative, concrete descriptions
At least one reference point is unexpected or non-obvious for the genre

assertion_results.note: All five sections plus AI Prompt Anchor present
Position statement describes 'the visual world of infrastructure journalism — steel, concrete, amber warning light'
Burtynsky is expected for industrial; surprise reference could be a graphic designer or typographer
Position creates specific literary-fiction visual world beyond 'intimate'
At least one non-obvious reference for literary fiction podcast
Describes 'the specific light of European border regions — grey winter in Finland, dusty warmth in Gibraltar'
Koudelka expected; Kaurismäki provides non-obvious angle for border documentary

methodology: Binary assertion testing: 3 inputs (standard, minimal, European context) x 3 assertions per skill. Each assertion graded pass/fail based on whether generated output satisfies the requirement.
